title: Fiery Smoked Irish Potato Hash
description: A crunchy and festive Irish dish featuring smoked potatoes, dijon mustard, and a spicy kick, perfect for a dairy-free friendly meal. This herbal and smoky hash is great for any time of day.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, toss the diced smoked potatoes with olive oil, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper until they are evenly coated.
Spread the potatoes out in a single layer on a baking sheet and roast in the oven for 20 minutes, or until they are crispy on the outside and tender on the inside.
While the potatoes are roasting, heat a large skillet over medium-high heat and add the diced onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
In a small bowl, whisk together the dijon mustard and a splash of water until smooth.
Add the roasted potatoes, mustard mixture, and cayenne pepper to the skillet with the onion and garlic, and stir until everything is well combined.
Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, until the hash is crispy and golden brown.
Stir in the chopped fresh parsley and serve hot.
